title: Hash or Secret? 🕵️
description: Identify which strings are cryptographic hashes and which are encrypted data.
problemStatement:
Look at these three strings found in a database. Based on their length and appearance, identify what they likely represent.

**String A**: `5e884898da28047151d0e56f8dc6292773603d0d6aabbdd62a11ef721d1542d8` (64 chars, hex)
**String B**: `U2FsdGVkX19Wkb5T+f7H4XQ9XJ...` (Starts with U2Fsd)
**String C**: `00-1A-2B-3C-4D-5E` (6 pairs separated by dashes)

**Your Mission**: Label them as **Hash**, **Encrypted Data**, or **MAC Address** in order.
hints: SHA-256 hashes are exactly 64 hexadecimal characters.
Commonly encrypted data often looks like base64-encoded strings.
MAC addresses have 6 pairs of characters.
expectedOutput:
A: Hash
B: Encrypted Data
C: MAC Address
solutionCode:
A: Hash
B: Encrypted Data
C: MAC Address
